summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall exfiltrates host information via child_process and HTTP
details: On install, postinstall.js loads child_process, os, and http, collects host identifiers including os.hostname() (lines 32, 45), and issues outbound network calls (curl invocations at lines 89-91 and http.request at line 51) that transmit the collected data to a remote endpoint. This is the canonical install-time host-reconnaissance / beacon pattern: any developer or build system running `npm install sea-bound-siren` automatically leaks host fingerprints to an attacker-controlled destination without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
